parsed.generic_mechanism: Belief change becomes more credible when the writer bounds the target, preserves autonomy, and converts inherited value claims into testable hypotheses; overbroad scope and absolute assurances can make a freedom message feel like imposed compliance.

paraphrased_evidence.A: A offers a withdrawal-relief cycle, conditioned taste and reward, poor satiety, and rebound hunger as explanations for repeated use. Those mechanisms can loosen belief, but sweeping analogies, unsupported universals, a very broad target, and compliance-heavy assurances erode trust and make freedom feel promised.
paraphrased_evidence.B: B makes the ongoing mental workload itself a cost, sharply limits what change would cover, protects normal eating and accidents, and reframes claimed rewards as inherited attributions open to testing. This creates credible curiosity and non-depriving escape, although direct tests of the benefits are postponed.

paraphrased_evidence.ours: B makes the ongoing mental workload itself a cost, sharply limits what change would cover, protects normal eating and accidents, and reframes claimed rewards as inherited attributions open to testing. This creates credible curiosity and non-depriving escape, although direct tests of the benefits are postponed.
paraphrased_evidence.ref: A offers a withdrawal-relief cycle, conditioned taste and reward, poor satiety, and rebound hunger as explanations for repeated use. Those mechanisms can loosen belief, but sweeping analogies, unsupported universals, a very broad target, and compliance-heavy assurances erode trust and make freedom feel promised.
